title: commiphora ornifolia var. glabra
intro: Commiphora ornifolia var. glabra is a variant of the Commiphora ornifolia plant, commonly known as the 'Himalayan Myrrh' or 'Indian Myrrh', a plant species native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Commiphora ornifolia var. glabra include wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, resin, and powder, which can be used in various forms such as topical creams, capsules, and teas.
